title: Kinder Scout (2) crew) Memorial
location_name: Kinder Scout (2), Peak District (Derbyshire / South Pennines)
crew_summary: Pilot Flt Lt. Aleksander Chelstowski killed
memorial_type: Crash site marker; engine and panels remain
narrative: Two Polish pilots on a flying exercise flew through cloud and struck the south end of Slight Side, near Scafell, in the Lake District. Both were killed. The Polish contribution to RAF operations during WW2 was substantial -- Polish squadrons flew with distinction in both Fighter and Bomber Command. Many Polish aircrew trained at OTUs across northern England, and the mountainous terrain of the Lake District claimed several training aircraft during the war years. The remote location of this crash, high on the Scafell range, meant the memorial was difficult to maintain, and the original deteriorated beyond repair over the decades.
cause_of_loss: Aircraft flew through cloud and struck mountain
memorial_details: The original memorial stone deteriorated over time due to the extreme weather conditions on the exposed fell. It was rededicated by 1030 Whitehaven Squadron Air Cadets, who arranged for a replacement memorial. The site is accessible to experienced fell walkers via routes from Wasdale Head or Eskdale.

external_narratives.summary: Research indicates this may relate to an Avro Anson crash on Kinder Scout involving Polish crew from a flying exercise on 11 December 1944. Flt Lt Aleksander Chelstowski took off from RAF Newton near Nottingham but became lost in low cloud. He descended below the cloud but struck the snow-covered moor. Remarkably, all crew survived the crash.
key_details: All crew survived according to some sources
Chelstowski found guilty of gross negligence but later decorated four times

external_narratives.summary: Comprehensive listing of Peak District aircraft crash sites including the Kinder Scout Polish crew incident. The blog documents engine and panel remains still visible at the site and provides grid references for walkers.
key_details: Engine and panels remain at crash site
